title: Allowing both safe and unsafe HTTP methods is security-sensitive
description: This rule checks if a class or function allows both safe (e.g., GET, HEAD) and unsafe (e.g., POST, PUT, DELETE) HTTP methods. Mixing safe and unsafe methods can lead to security vulnerabilities.
message: A class or function allows both safe and unsafe HTTP methods.
recommendation: Consider restricting HTTP methods to only safe ones.

noncompliant.description: A class that allows both GET and POST methods.

compliant.description: A class that only allows GET methods.

why_is_this_an_issue: Mixing safe and unsafe HTTP methods can lead to security vulnerabilities like information disclosure, integrity issues, and unauthorized access.
how_to_fix: Restrict HTTP methods to only safe ones.
